text:
Germanheptathlete Sophie Weissenberg sobbed uncontrollably after suffering a freak injury moments before competing at the ParisOlympics.

The 26-year-old was going through her final preparations on the Stade de France running track when she tripped over a hurdle and appeared to injure her ankle.

The cameras cut to Weissenberg lying in a heap on the ground and receiving treatment from multiple members of theParis2024 medical team.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Clearly aware that her Olympics were over, the German punched the floor in frustration as she sobbed with her face planted onto the track.

Replays showed Weissenberg tripping ahead of her 110m hurdle race in the women’s heptathlon event and then rolling her ankle and falling to the floor.

Weissenberg was in tears as she was helped up by several medics and had her head in her hands as she was taken out of the arena by a wheelchair.

‘I missed that, I was looking down but I missed that,’ BBC commentator Steve Cram said.

‘She was hopping from hitting the hurdle, that could be an ankle. She definitely turned her foot.’

‘You can imagine how bitterly disappointed she will be,’ another pundit said.

‘You think you’ve done everything to prepare and something minor happens on your final preparations.’

Weissenberg, who is ranked ninth in the world, pulled out of the competition following the injury blow, with the German Athletics Association confirming she suffered a torn Achilles tendon in her left foot.

Team GB medal hope Katarina Johnson-Thompson made a strong start to the heptathlon, leading the competition after the 110m hurdle and high jump events.

‘Katerina is in a good place, clearly. She is fit and ready for this,’ former Great Britain runner Allison Curbishley said on BBC Radio 5 Live.

‘The year that she won the world title, she cleared 1.95m. She’s ahead of Thiam, the Olympic champion, ahead of the shotput, which is one of her weaker disciplines.

‘I expect there will be a little to and fro, and she may not be in the gold medal position by the start of 200m later on, but that is where she can claw back plenty of points.

‘I am really hopeful for her. This looks like really good potential for an Olympic medal.’

title: Athlete sobs uncontrollably after suffering Olympic heartbreak moments before competing | Metro News
description: German heptathlete Sophie Weissenberg sobbed uncontrollably after suffering a freak injury moments before competing at the Paris Olympics.
